name: Alley cropping
definition: Planted rows of woody perennials with annual crops cultivated in the alleys between them.
dominant_limiting_factor: Arable biophysical envelope + machinery/management access on cultivable land.
grouping_rationale: Planted silvoarable systems on cultivable land share a slope/soil/rainfall envelope and a management-access constraint; grouped by that shared limiting factor, not by tree species.

name: Tree intercropping
definition: Planted trees dispersed within or among annual crops on cultivable land.
dominant_limiting_factor: Arable biophysical envelope + management access on cultivable land.
grouping_rationale: Same planted-on-cropland envelope as alley cropping; same dominant limiting factor.

name: Planted boundary / live fence on cropland
definition: Deliberately planted tree/shrub lines on the boundaries of cultivated fields.
dominant_limiting_factor: Arable biophysical envelope + management access; established by planting.
grouping_rationale: Planted establishment on cropland places it in the silvoarable envelope family (distinct from F4, where the limiting factor is linear placement geometry rather than the arable envelope).

name: Farmer-managed natural regeneration (FMNR)
definition: Re-establishing tree cover by protecting and managing natural regrowth from living stumps, roots and seed, rather than planting.
dominant_limiting_factor: Regeneration potential — remnant rootstock, seedbank, seed-source proximity, and tenure security to protect regrowth.
grouping_rationale: Limited by regeneration potential, not the planting envelope; a different variable set (woody-cover baseline/change, seed-source distance, tenure), so it cannot be an override of F1.

name: Parkland tree retention
definition: Retaining/regenerating scattered mature trees on cropland (e.g. Faidherbia parkland) by protecting natural regeneration.
dominant_limiting_factor: Regeneration potential + tenure (retention/ANR), not deliberate planting.
grouping_rationale: Retained parkland is regeneration-driven (kin to ANR/FMNR); a planted parkland would instead sit in F1. Keyed by establishment driver, not appearance.

name: Assisted natural regeneration on farmland
definition: Active assistance (weeding, protection, thinning) to accelerate natural regeneration on farmland.
dominant_limiting_factor: Regeneration potential — seedbank/rootstock, seed source, tenure.
grouping_rationale: Same regeneration-potential limiting factor as FMNR and parkland retention.

name: Planted silvopasture
definition: Deliberate integration of planted trees with pasture and grazing livestock.
dominant_limiting_factor: Biophysical envelope PLUS grazing context and forage/livestock variables.
grouping_rationale: Needs additional variables (forage, grazing pressure, livestock) beyond the silvoarable envelope; overrides only re-parameterise existing variables, so it is its own family.

name: Agrosilvopasture
definition: Combined trees + crops + livestock on the same land unit.
grouping_rationale: Shares the grazing-context limiting factor with planted silvopasture.

name: Windbreaks & shelterbelts
definition: Tree/shrub lines positioned to reduce wind exposure across fields.
dominant_limiting_factor: Linear-feature geometry — wind exposure and field-boundary placement.
grouping_rationale: Placement (line within a candidate zone) is the limiting logic, not arable envelope; footprint reported as candidate length/density, never pixel area.

name: Contour & vegetative buffers
definition: Tree/shrub lines along contours or slope-flow lines to reduce erosion and runoff.
dominant_limiting_factor: Linear-feature geometry — slope/flow lines + boundary placement.
grouping_rationale: Same linear-placement limiting factor as windbreaks; candidate length/density footprint.

name: Shaded perennial-crop system
definition: Shade trees grown over an existing perennial cash crop. The understorey crop (coffee, cocoa, tea, cardamom, pepper, ...) is a PARAMETER of the family, scoped per AOI - not a separate subpractice.
dominant_limiting_factor: The host crop's observed distribution / production (where the crop is actually grown), plus shade-as-adaptation at the climate-stressed edge.
grouping_rationale: One family, crop as a parameter (run per candidate crop, combine by max retaining the driving crop) - the way F1 is not split by tree species. Gated by the crop's observed distribution (MapSPAM / GAEZ / EO commodity maps / ag-stats), not a modelled niche; bioclimatic-envelope modelling is the fallback only where distribution data are absent. Crops are not blended into one envelope (coffee highlands and cocoa lowlands are nearly disjoint).

name: Multistrata homegardens
definition: Intensive multistrata tree-crop-livestock gardens adjacent to dwellings.
dominant_limiting_factor: Settlement and water proximity at sub-pixel scale.
grouping_rationale: Sub-pixel and proximity-driven; a raster suitability surface misleads, so handled qualitatively via the scorecard + M6 note rather than mapped.

name: Macro-catchment water harvesting
definition: Structures that collect runoff from a catchment larger than the cultivated/storage area (e.g. check dams, farm ponds).
dominant_limiting_factor: Runoff generation + storable terrain (slope, drainage, soil permeability).
grouping_rationale: Runoff-catchment WH practices share a runoff-generation + storage-terrain limiting factor; reported as an applicability zone, not pixel area.
